qoq_changes: Deferred net revenue increased significantly to $2,490 million from $1,700 million at the end of the prior fiscal year (March 31, 2025).
Cash and cash equivalents grew to $2,784 million from $2,136 million at March 31, 2025.
new_risks: Severe margin compression risk emerged this quarter, as surging R&D ($704M vs $606M YoY) and Marketing ($356M vs $251M YoY) expenses drove a 70% decline in net income despite flat top-line revenue.
management_tone: Neutral; while GAAP profitability took a significant hit from rising operating costs, strong cash flow generation and deferred revenue growth indicate underlying franchise health.
analyst_note: EA's quarter reflects heavy investment in R&D and marketing, which crushed GAAP net income (-70% YoY) despite stable revenue. However, a massive $790M build in deferred revenue and nearly $2B in 9-month operating cash flow suggest strong bookings momentum that will convert to recognized revenue in future periods.
